prose:
Method provenance
• Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, f⁰, dual descriptor) and identification of the most electrophilic (largest f⁺) and most nucleophilic (largest f⁻) atoms in benzaldehyde.
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’); exact four-center integrals (density fitting not invoked).
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (`benzaldehyde.xyz`); single-point charges for N, N+1, and N−1 states – no further geometry relaxation (DFT//input-XYZ).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model).
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation and anion both treated as doublets (charge ±1, multiplicity 2).
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend: PySCF 2.x).
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity status “ok”, trustworthy = true.  
  Warnings reported by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Key results (per-atom Mulliken-based Fukui indices; atom labels refer to the input ordering)
  – Most electrophilic site (max f⁺): atom 3 (C) f⁺ = 0.39938967  
  – Most nucleophilic site (max f⁻): atom 1 (O) f⁻ = 0.23839906  

The headline value provided above (0.39938967) is the largest condensed electrophilic Fukui index f⁺ for benzaldehyde under the stated computational conditions.

All warnings from the tool (verbatim):
• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)  
• common header present  

Live log & files
The calculation’s live PySCF output was streamed to a `.out` file announced at run start (available for inspection; tail with `tail -f`). The full JSON result is saved as `benzaldehyde_fukui_dft.json`, and the bar-chart of Fukui indices is at the path in the `plot` field of that JSON. All artifacts are retained for reproducibility.
